## What are ADRs in SSOT Registry?
URL: https://ssot-registry.com/features/developer/adrs/what-is/
Description: ADRs matter because SSOT Registry turns them into named, normalized, inspectable records instead of leaving developers to reconstruct intent from scattered documents. This features guide explains the concept, shows where it fits in the registry, and points to practical next steps.

## How to use ADRs in SSOT Registry
URL: https://ssot-registry.com/features/developer/adrs/how-to-use/
Description: developers use ADRs by reading or changing the registry entity, linking it to adjacent work, and validating the result before downstream automation or release review depends on it. URL: https://ssot-registry.com/features/developer/claims/what-is/

## What are Issues in SSOT Registry?
URL: https://ssot-registry.com/features/developer/issues/what-is/

## What are ADRs in SSOT Registry?
URL: https://ssot-registry.com/glossary/developer/adrs/definition/
Description: ADRs matter because SSOT Registry turns them into named, normalized, inspectable records instead of leaving developers to reconstruct intent from scattered documents. This glossary guide explains the concept, shows where it fits in the registry, and points to practical next steps.

## What are Operator guides in SSOT Registry?
URL: https://ssot-registry.com/glossary/developer/operator-guides/definition/
Description: Operator guides matter because SSOT Registry turns them into named, normalized, inspectable records instead of leaving developers to reconstruct intent from scattered documents. This glossary guide explains the concept, shows where it fits in the registry, and points to practical next steps.
